title: ClusterConfig
description: Universal input contract for every Pilot tool (`pilot.tools.*`). Declares the runtime environment Pilot operates inside. Two modes: `single` (a prepared single-node container) or `slurm` (an existing SLURM allocation; the user has already run `salloc`/`sbatch`). Pilot reads this file once per invocation and uses it to derive the LaunchPlan; it never reads ssh hostnames, k8s manifests, or docker run arguments. Anything outside this schema is the user's pre-Pilot prep responsibility. See `skills/workflow/preflight.md` §3 and `AGENTS.md` §X (universal tool contract).

schema_version.description: Schema version. Currently only `1.0` is accepted; bumped on breaking changes.

cluster_id.description: Stable, human-readable identifier of this cluster. Used as the namespace for `state/cluster_profiles/<cluster_id>_*.yaml` and other artifacts. Two cluster.yaml files with the same cluster_id MUST point at the same physical cluster (otherwise caches will collide).

mode.description: Runtime mode. `single`: Pilot runs entirely inside the current container, sees N local GPUs, never spawns peer-node processes. `slurm`: Pilot is allowed to call `srun --jobid=<slurm.job_id>` to fan out to the nodes already allocated by the user; it never calls `salloc`/`sbatch` itself.

slurm.description: Required when `mode == slurm`. Describes the user's pre-existing SLURM allocation. Pilot only attaches to it via `srun --jobid=...`; it does not create or destroy allocations.

job_id.description: SLURM job id of the allocation Pilot should attach to. Pilot validates with `scontrol show job <job_id>` and aborts if the job is not in RUNNING state.

nnodes.description: Optional override of node count. When omitted, Pilot reads `NumNodes` from `scontrol show job`. Must match the live allocation; mismatch → `failure.kind=CLUSTER`.

nodelist.description: Optional override of SLURM nodelist string (e.g. `smc[01-04]`). When omitted, derived from `scontrol show job`. Used to compute the rdzv head node = first hostname in expanded list.

partition.description: Optional partition annotation (info only; Pilot does not pass it to srun).

rdzv_port.description: Port used for torchrun rendezvous on the head node. Must be free on the head node and reachable from peers. Default 29400.

single.description: Optional block when `mode == single`. Currently has no required fields; reserved for future single-node knobs (e.g. local-rank limits).

max_local_gpus.description: Optional cap on number of GPUs Pilot uses on this single node (e.g. when sharing the host with another job). When omitted, all visible GPUs are used. `HIP_VISIBLE_DEVICES`/`CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES` still take precedence.

runtime.description: Soft annotations describing the container/runtime Pilot is executing inside. None of these fields are required; they are recorded into ClusterProfile metadata and used as human-readable suffixes in cache filenames. They do NOT participate in cache invalidation — that is driven by the auto-collected `runtime_fingerprint` (rocm/torch/rccl versions) inside ClusterProfile.

image_label.description: Short, filesystem-safe label for the container image (e.g. `rocm7.2-torch2.10`). Appears as a suffix in ClusterProfile filenames so humans can tell profiles apart at a glance.

notes.description: Free-text human note (e.g. `bring-up cluster, expect 5% degradation`). Surfaced verbatim in ClusterProfile.metadata.cluster_config_notes.

then.errorMessage: When mode=slurm, the `slurm` block (with at least `job_id`) is required.
When mode=single, the `slurm` block must be absent.
